entry.title: Balance Confirmations form is not showing correct id
entry.content:
Account Statement with Due Date List form is not displaying credit memo id correctly for the Balance Confirmations.  


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Balance Confirmations form is not showing correct id
entry.content:
Credit Memo has been created as a follow up of the Invoice, and that is the reason why the system displays the Invoice ID instead of Credit Memo ID.
For Credit Memos created as a follow up of Customer Invoices the system will always show the Invoice ID in the Balance Confirmation PDF.

If the Credit Memo is not created as a follow up of an invoice, then system displays the Credit Memo ID in the Balance Confirmation PDF.

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP(RESTful Application Programming) Model OData V2 API Deep Entity
entry.content:
And what would this achieve? You do POST and what happens? Unless there is something not shown, I assume it will just do... nothing.

You've mentioned this is needed on "a lot of project". I struggle to understand why. What purpose could this possibly serve? Sure, you could add the code that actually does something (this is unmanaged implementation, choice is not explained though). But that code is what's interesting. Defining entity is boilerplate. And it's the same process that's been covered by countless other blogs and tutorials. You've skipped SELECT part but that's all. But this goes back to previous question - what any of this is actually meant to do? I'm very confused...

entry.title: Protecting SAP RISE Customer Data - Building on AWS Security Capabilities
entry.content:
By Nicolas Berthier - SAP Specialty SA (AWS), Gerald Boyne - Principal Security & Compliance Strategist (AWS), Koen Claesen - Cloud Security & Compliance Advisor (SAP), Geroen Joris - Specialist Security SA (AWS), Mark Steen - Cloud Security & Compliance Advisor (SAP),  Jay Thoden Van Velzen - Technical Advisor, Office of the CSO (SAP)

RISE with SAP provides an attractive solution for organizations to deploy their S/4 HANA solutions in a private cloud environment, available on various public cloud provider platforms, including Amazon Web Services (AWS). The security and data privacy of RISE landscapes and the business operations they support are critical and must be safeguarded. Elsewhere, you can learn more about how SAP protects RISE and cloud landscapes generally. In this article, we describe security measures that prevent access by AWS into SAP-managed cloud accounts.

 

Shared Responsibility Model and Access Boundaries

In cloud security, cloud providers and their customers operate on a Shared Responsibility Model. In case of AWS that is described here. With RISE with SAP on AWS, that responsibility extends to three parties.

AWS provides i.e. compute, network and storage services, but expects SAP to configure them securely. SAP expects customers to manage their end of the network connectivity to the RISE private cloud, the security of users, authorizations, and business processes within their cloud solution instance.

Simply put, AWS is responsible for the security and compliance 'of' the cloud and implements security controls to secure AWS services and hosts. SAP Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS) determines, designs and implements the security and compliance controls required for RISE operations, and is responsible for security ‘in’ the cloud. Customers are responsible for the security ‘in’ the RISE application.

The separation of responsibilities is depicted in the diagram below.

Shared Responsibility Model diagram for SAP Cloud Services running on public cloud infrastructure

The separation between the three parties is to preserve strict privacy and confidentiality boundaries to keep workloads and data protected from other customers, AWS and SAP.

 

Secure-by-Design Access Protections

Contracts between customers, SAP and AWS include data privacy and confidentiality clauses. These commitments are also expressed in security and operational processes that are periodically audited by third-party assessors. Both AWS and SAP publish such attestations and certifications.

However, these access boundaries are also enforced through strong technical controls. Three controls that prevent AWS from accessing SAP landscapes and customer data are:

  • AWS Nitro for confidential computing
  • Encryption keys controlled by SAP or customer
  • Secure backups

AWS Nitro

RISE on AWS is currently deployed on Nitro-based Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2) instances. Traditionally, hypervisors protect the physical hardware and bios, virtualize the CPU, storage, networking, and provide a rich set of management capabilities. With the Nitro System, these functions are broken apart and offloaded to dedicated hardware and software. In doing so, this reduces costs by delivering practically all the resources of a server to your instances. This provides performance benefits, but also increases the security and isolation of virtualized resources.

The core component responsible for managing the lifecycle and operation of guest virtual machines (VMs) in a virtualization system are called a virtual machine monitor (VMM), or hypervisor. Hypervisors typically employ a general-purpose operating system to interface with a variety of system hardware, run device models, and run other management software for the virtualization system. This operating system is commonly implemented as a special privileged virtual machine (Dom0 in Xen, system root partition in Hyper-V). The introduction of the Nitro System consisted of an incremental decomposition of the software

components running in Dom0 on a general-purpose data center CPU into independent purpose-built service processor units. This has eliminated the need for Dom0 on an EC2 instance. Instead, a custom-developed, minimized hypervisor based on KVM provides a lightweight VMM, while offloading other functions such as those previously performed by the device-models in Dom0 into a set of discrete Nitro Cards. This Nitro Hypervisor is a limited and carefully designed component that has been intentionally minimized and purpose built with the capabilities needed to perform its assigned functions, and no more: it includes no networking stack, no general-purpose file system, and no peripheral device driver support. It doesn't contain a shell or any type of interactive access mode.

Diagram illustrating the difference between 'classical' virtualization and the AWS Nitro System, and dedicated hardware Nitro cardsBy design the Nitro System has no operator access. There is no mechanism for any system or person to log in to EC2 Nitro hosts, access the memory of EC2 instances, or access any customer data stored on local encrypted instance storage or remote encrypted EBS volumes. If any AWS operator, including those with the highest privileges, needs to perform maintenance work on an EC2 server, they can only use a limited set of authenticated, authorized, logged, and audited administrative APIs. None of these APIs provide an operator the ability to access customer data on the EC2 server. Because these are designed and tested technical restrictions built into the Nitro System itself, no AWS operator can bypass these controls and protections.

Independent security experts at NCC Group reviewed the operator access controls and confirmed there is no way for AWS personnel to remotely log in to the underlying physical server.

AWS contractually confirms that “AWS personnel do not have access to Your Content on AWS Nitro System EC2 instances.”

More details on the Nitro System can be found in the AWS documentation.

Encryption

Strong cryptographic techniques are another tool to protect data across privacy and security boundaries. The Nitro System provides encryption at-rest, in-transit, and in-memory.

At Rest

Amazon Elastic Block Storage (EBS) volumes are encrypted with data encryption keys that are unique to each volume. These are used to encrypt and decrypt data. This data key is itself encrypted and that encrypted key is stored in the FIPS 140-2 Level 3 certified AWS Key Management Service (KMS) and protected by an access key. Access to the access key is governed by authorizations within the scope and control of SAP cloud administrators. SAP administrators and systems must have the Decryptauthorization and must have access to the particular volume within the particular customer landscape to access the data. For data operations, AWS back-end processes act on the administrator’s or system’s behalf, so are always initiated by SAP.

Simplified diagram illustrating how storage managed by SAP is encrypted in a way that SAP operators have access to the data, but AWS operators do not

SAP doesn’t have user access into customer cloud solutions. AWS staff, similarly, don't have access to SAP-managed cloud landscapes. Since AWS doesn't have identities or authorizations in SAP-managed cloud accounts, they have no access to the access keys required for the encrypted data keys that provide access to SAP EBS volumes. The access restriction for the operator is validated by an independent third party und FIPS 140-2 level 3 criteria.

This is a simplified description. More details on EBS volume encryption in the Nitro System are described in AWS documentation.

In Transit

AWS provides secure and private connectivity between EC2 instances. Instance types used by RISE with SAP leverage the underlying Nitro System hardware to automatically encrypt in-transit traffic between instances in the environment.

More information is described in the AWS documentation.

Note that this encryption in-transit is in addition to any TLS and VPN connections deployed by SAP.

In Memory

RISE on AWS is currently deployed on Nitro-based EC2 instances that utilize CPU chip level DRAM encryption from Intel's Total Memory Encryption (TME) technology. Each instance running on an Intel TME powered host uses individual cyphers. Intel TME protects against hardware or physical attacks on system memory, such as cold boot attacks.

More information can be found in this Intel TME white paper.

Secure Backups

RISE with SAP attaches an EBS volume to every virtual machine that it deploys on AWS, as described above under encryption at-rest. To keep customer data readily recoverable, SAP relies on Amazon EBS Snapshots. The snapshots are managed and stored on AWS Simple Storage Service (AWS S3). The storage buckets are encrypted with SSE-KMS-CMK (customer-managed keys, held in AWS KMS). The snapshots are encrypted, and any SAP HANA data files contained within them are encrypted with HANA encryption keys.

Finally, these snapshots are protected by EBS Snapshot Lock, to protect them against accidental or malicious deletions. While a snapshot is locked, it can't be deleted by any user, regardless of their IAM permissions. 

 

Conclusion

RISE with SAP on AWS provides a robust and secure cloud environment, leveraging the advanced security features of the AWS Nitro System. Through prevention of system access, to encryption at-rest, in-transit, and in-processing, along with cryptographic key protection and secure backup, SAP and AWS jointly ensure that customer data remains protected.

AWS’ and SAP’s commitment to security and privacy, combined with its rigorous compliance with international standards and independent validations, offers strong assurances to customers concerned about data exposure.

The shared responsibility model between AWS, SAP, and customers enhances the security posture of RISE landscapes, ensuring reliable service and strict adherence to data protection standards.

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
How to create a Custom Business Configurations app that used the strict mode 2
entry.content:
Introduction

Recently we got a customer influence request with the ask to release the RAP transport API’s so that the same can be used in RAP business objects that use the strict mode 2.

An example of such a RAP business object is a Business Configuration Maintenance Object that has been generated using the ABAP Repository Object Generator.

When you just change the behavior definition and the behavior projection such that they use the strict mode 2 instead of the basic strict mode 

 

 

 

managed with additional save implementation in class ZBP_I_HOLIDAYCAL2_S unique;
//strict;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;

 

 

 

and

 

 

 

projection implementation in class ZBP_C_HOLIDAYCAL2_S unique;
//strict;
strict ( 2 );
use draft;

 

 

 

we would get the following dump when trying to save the data to a customizing request:

Statement "EXEC SQL COMMIT" is not allowed with this status.

Since making the existing RAP transport API's compatible with the strict mode 2 is probably a task that cannot be achieved at all together with colleagues I thought about possible workarounds.

We came up with the idea to wrap the calls of the transport API's which cause the trouble using the background processing framework.

This can be achieved by some small changes to the save_modified method of the behavior implementation class and by providing a wrapper class that implements the interfaces of the background processing framework.

So let’s get started.

Generate a business configuration maintenance object

For this blog post I have used the tables listed at the end of the blog post and the generator for business configuration maintenance objects which is described in this tutorial

Create Business Configuration Maintenance Object | SAP Tutorials

In addition one has to create appropriate authorizations for the CDS view that will be generated (here ZI_HolidayCal2)  rah

 

 

 

Changes in the behavior implementation class

In the behavior implementation class we have to adapt the save_modified( ) method and we have to add types definitions in the global behavior class which can be used by our wrapper class.

Local saver class

In the save_modified method we comment out the call of the local class lhc_rap_tdat_cts which contains the call of the RAP transport API.

Instead we are using a class called zcl_record_changes_via_bgpf which implements the interfaces if_serializable_object, if_bgmc_operation and if_bgmc_op_single_tx_uncontr.

The latter allows us to call API’s that may contain commit work statements which would cause a dump when being called directly in the behavior implementation. Instead this class acts as wrapper and and starts the call of the transport API asynchronously in a new process.

 

 

CLASS lsc_zi_holidaycal2_s DEFINITION FINAL INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_saver.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
    METHODS:
      save_modified REDEFINITION,
      cleanup_finalize REDEFINITION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS lsc_zi_holidaycal2_s IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD save_modified.

    READ TABLE update-HolidayCal2All INDEX 1 INTO DATA(all).
    IF all-TransportRequestID IS NOT INITIAL.

      "start new implementation

      DATA bgpf_operation TYPE REF TO zcl_record_changes_via_bgpf .

      bgpf_operation = NEW zcl_record_changes_via_bgpf(
        i_transport_request = all-TransportRequestID
        i_create            = create
        i_update            = update
        i_delete            = delete
      ).
      TRY.
          DATA(background_process) = cl_bgmc_process_factory=>get_default(  )->create(  ).
          background_process->set_operation_tx_uncontrolled( bgpf_operation ).
          background_process->set_name( CONV #( all-TransportRequestID ) ).
          background_process->save_for_execution(  ).
        CATCH cx_bgmc.
          "handle exception
          ASSERT 1 = 2.
      ENDTRY.

      "end new implementation

*      lhc_rap_tdat_cts=>get( )->record_changes(
*                                  transport_request = all-TransportRequestID
*                                  create            = REF #( create )
*                                  update            = REF #( update )
*                                  delete            = REF #( delete ) ).

    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD cleanup_finalize ##NEEDED.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

 

 

Global class

In the global class we add three type statements so that we can reuse the same in the constructor of our wrapper class.

 

 

CLASS zbp_i_holidaycal2_s DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  ABSTRACT
  FINAL
  FOR BEHAVIOR OF zi_holidaycal2_s .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    TYPES : t_create TYPE REQUEST FOR CHANGE zi_holidaycal2_s,
            t_update TYPE REQUEST FOR CHANGE zi_holidaycal2_s,
            t_delete type request for delete zi_holidaycal2_s .
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zbp_i_holidaycal2_s IMPLEMENTATION.
ENDCLASS.

 

 

 

Wrapper class zcl_record_changes_via_bgpf

The wrapper class contains fairly generic coding. The constructor takes the three complex structures which contains the data which shall be written to our customizing request.

Since the class will be serialized by the background processing framework I have made sure that the constructor does not take the references but the actual values of the tables that are passed by the behavior implementation.

 

 

 

CLASS zcl_record_changes_via_bgpf DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    INTERFACES if_serializable_object .
    INTERFACES if_bgmc_operation .
    INTERFACES if_bgmc_op_single_tx_uncontr .




    METHODS constructor
      IMPORTING i_transport_request TYPE sxco_transport
                i_create            TYPE zbp_i_holidaycal2_s=>t_create
                i_update            TYPE zbp_i_holidaycal2_s=>t_update
                i_delete            TYPE zbp_i_holidaycal2_s=>t_delete.


  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.

    DATA : transport_request TYPE sxco_transport,
           create            TYPE zbp_i_holidaycal2_s=>t_create,
           update            TYPE zbp_i_holidaycal2_s=>t_update,
           delete            TYPE zbp_i_holidaycal2_s=>t_delete.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS zcl_record_changes_via_bgpf IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD constructor.
    create = i_create.
    update = i_update.
    delete = i_delete.
    transport_request = i_transport_request.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD if_bgmc_op_single_tx_uncontr~execute.

    lhc_rap_tdat_cts=>get( )->record_changes(
                                transport_request = transport_request
                                create            = REF #( create )
                                update            = REF #( update )
                                delete            = REF #( delete ) ).

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

 

 

Local class LHC_RAP_TDAT_CTS

Our wrapper class zcl_record_changes_via_bgpf uses the same code as the one that we have commented out in the save_modified( ) method of the generated behavior implementation.

We have thus to create a local class LHC_RAP_TDAT_CTS in our global class zcl_record_changes_via_bgpf as well.

 

 

CLASS LHC_RAP_TDAT_CTS DEFINITION FINAL.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS:
      GET
        RETURNING
          VALUE(RESULT) TYPE REF TO IF_MBC_CP_RAP_TDAT_CTS.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS LHC_RAP_TDAT_CTS IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD GET.
    result = mbc_cp_api=>rap_tdat_cts( tdat_name = 'ZHOLIDAYCAL2'
                                       table_entity_relations = VALUE #(
                                         ( entity = 'HolidayCal2' table = 'ZBC_FCAL_HOLI' )
                                         ( entity = 'HolidayCal2Text' table = 'ZBC_FCAL_HOLI_T' )
                                       ) ) ##NO_TEXT.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

 

Result

When we now start the business configuration app and enter data and save the same,

this data can be written to customizing request that we have to create beforehand.

 

Tables used

For this demo I used the following tables and generated a business configuration maintenance object based on these two tables

zbc_fcal_holi

 

@EndUserText.label : 'Holiday Cal2'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #C
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #ALLOWED
define table zbc_fcal_holi {

  key client            : mandt not null;
  key holiday_id        : zdmo_fcal_holiday_id not null;
  month_of_holiday      : fdt_month;
  day_of_holiday        : fdt_day_of_month;
  configdeprecationcode : config_deprecation_code;
  last_changed_at       : abp_lastchange_tstmpl;
  local_last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;

}

 

zbc_fcal_holi_t

 

@EndUserText.label : 'Holiday Cal2 Text Table'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #C
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #ALLOWED
define table zbc_fcal_holi_t {

  key client            : mandt not null;
  @AbapCatalog.textLanguage
  key lang              : abap.lang not null;
  @AbapCatalog.foreignKey.keyType : #TEXT_KEY
  @AbapCatalog.foreignKey.screenCheck : false
  key holiday_id        : zdmo_fcal_holiday_id not null
    with foreign key [0..*,1] zbc_fcal_holi
      where client = zbc_fcal_holi_t.client
        and holiday_id = zbc_fcal_holi_t.holiday_id;
  fcal_description      : sxco_ao_short_description;
  local_last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;

}

Semantic Onboarding allows you to transfer/import the models into Datasphere, allowing you to consume immediately, however the data is not transferred/replicated. You may switch on the "remote table replication" to populate the remote tables.

entry.title: Record in lookup table data ignored during subscription price calculation - Subscription billing
entry.content:
Hi All,

I noticed following behavior while setting up pricing in Subscription billing.

I uploaded following records for RecurringPrice code in lookup table data:

productCodemarketIdrateElementValidity StartValidity Endprice (Price Condition Type)price (Range Value)price (Range UoM or Currency)price (Price Type)price (Amount Value or Multiplier)price (Amount Currency)price (Pricing Unit Value)price (Pricing Unit UoM or Currency)
P-SUBSCRIPTIONXX00RTEST2024-01-019999-12-31Single  Linear45EUR1EA
P-SUBSCRIPTION(none)RTEST2024-01-019999-12-31Single  Linear50EUR1EA

When I create a subscription for market XX00 and product P-SUBSCRIPTION, price 45€/ea is correctly determined.

While when I create a subscription for another market and product P-SUBSCRIPTION, price 50€/each is not determined, no price is determined at all for the recurring charge.

Is it correct then to say that even if no error is generated when a lookup table data file is imported containing records for an incomplete combination of lookup table fields (in my case I left the field marketId empty) the record is considered invalid by the system and is ignored during pricing calculation?
In other words, lookup table fields in lookup table data cannot be left empty to be used as wildcards and in my example if I want to set prices for all markets I should create a new lookup table that doesn't contain the field marketId?

Than you in advance for helping me understand how Subscription billing works.

KR,
Elena

entry.title: Range prompt parameter hyperlink in Web Intelligence report
entry.content:
My range prompt parameter hyperlink that was working on 

SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform 4.3 Support Pack 2 Patch 10 Version : 14.3.2.4537.10

is broken in 

SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform 4.3 Support Pack 4 Patch 6 Version : 14.3.4.5014.6

The working hyperlink was

="<a href=\"../../opendoc/openDocument.jsp?iDocID="+URLEncode("AYSqscJEsZ9LrFFwE5ku22c")+"&sIDType="+URLEncode("CUID")+"&sType="+URLEncode("wid")+"&lsSZSM_PROJECT2="+URLEncode("" +UserResponse([LabFac];"ZSM_PROJECT2";Index))+"&lsRZRM_FYEAR="+URLEncode("" +Replace(UserResponse([LabFac];"ZRM_FYEAR";Index);" - ";".."))+"&sWindow="+URLEncode("Same")+"\" target=\"_self\" nav=\"doc\">"+HTMLEncode("" +[LabFac].[Project Definition].[Project Definition - Key] + " - " + [LabFac].[Project Definition].[Project Definition - Text])+"</a>"

It looks like some items are deprecated in the new version but I tried a few variations and none of them seem to work.

In the new version I tried this, but it's not working. When I remove the fiscal year range bit, it seems to be okay.

="<a href=\"../../opendoc/openDocument.jsp?iDocID="+URLEncode("AYSqscJEsZ9LrFFwE5ku22c")+"&sIDType="+URLEncode("CUID")+"&sRefresh="+URLEncode("Y")+"&lsSProject%20Definition="+URLEncode("" +UserResponse([LabFac];"ZSM_PROJECT2";Index))+"&lsRFiscal%20Year%20Range%3A%20E.g.%20(2000%20-%202010)="+URLEncode("" +Replace(UserResponse([LabFac];"ZRM_FYEAR";Index);" - ";".."))+"\" target=\"_self\" nav=\"doc\">"+HTMLEncode("" +[LabFac].[Project Definition].[Project Definition - Key] + " - " + [LabFac].[Project Definition].[Project Definition - Text])+"</a>"

The resulting link URL it creates is the following. It doesn't work in new patch but works in old patch

link/BOE/OpenDocument/opendoc/openDocument.jsp?iDocID=AYSqscJEsZ9LrFFwE5ku22c&sIDType=CUID&lsSZSM_PROJECT2=A10198610000000000&lsRZRM_FYEAR=2022%2E%2E2022

How do I pass in a range prompt in the new version?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Issues with an INT64 parameter in a Com UFL..
entry.content: Yes, Don, I did read the KB. I believe I am using the SQL Expression for its indended purpose. Basically I want to perform my database function TOSTRING on a column for every row returned in the query. That works fine when I execute the report for real. My where clause is applied, a limited number of rows come back, and ToString is performed on my column for every row. Perfect. Did you understand what I claim the issue is? Simply having this expression in my report results in a full table read when I load the report into the designer. No where clause is applied, not even the one in my record selection criteria. My table has potentially millions of rows.

entry.title: Re: In OPD I'm trying to find the email recipient table i...
entry.content:
Hello team,

SAP reserves the right to not disclose information about its internal tables, how it stores the data and processes it.
The BRF+ consists of a complex framework and only the developers and some support engineers know exactly where the data is stored and processed.

You might achieve what you want using standard processes like exporting the table to excel from time to time and saving the email receipient information.

Best regards,
Eduardo

entry.title: Re: Issues with an INT64 parameter in a Com UFL..
entry.content:
Most likely, your record selection is using a Crystal function, forcing the filtering to be done by Crystal rather than by the DBMS. Google the topic of "optimizing Crystal Reports" and you'll see various tips on how to avoid that problem. 

Since you are familiar with creating UFLs, you could implement the SQL conversion using a formula that uses a UFL function that calls the SQL statement and returns the result of the conversion. 
Ken Hamady maintains a listing of 3rd-party UFLs here.  My CUT Light UFL provides such an SQL function.

entry.title: Re: Issues with an INT64 parameter in a Com UFL..

When I use the preview from the design, again it is all sent to the database. But the initial load of the report in the designer uses no filtering. As for the UFL function, this reminds me of the song ' theres a hole in my bucket'. I originally wanted to do this with a UFL function pass the column value directly. The column is an INT64, thus the original issue rears its ugly head: i can't create a UFL function that takes an int64 param. I'm going to work around these apparently Crystal issues by adding a new column to the table which will be a string representation of the INT64 column. Ugly, but resolves my issue.

entry.title: Re: Multi level make to order MTO production orders
entry.content:
I really dont think SAP provides this functionality. So I am just trying different settings to get my result, with no success 

What I really need it collective production orders, MTO, where I have one "parent"/superior" order and multiple "child" orders all associated to the same sales order. See table CAUFV-MAUFNR. The data relationship provides the top down linking, Where, if I was programming this, you could cascade the sales order number down to all the child production orders. But collective orders only allows for one child order to a superior order. The main problem is the inability to group one (superior) to many child production orders.

entry.title: Readiness Check Run for S/4HANA "FOUNDATION" System Upgrade to 2023
entry.content:
Hi All,

Need advise on SAP Readiness Check  run for upgrade to 2023 on-prem version of S/4HANA "FOUNDATION" only system. As per SAP Note# 3049197 it says that Readiness Check is only applicable for S/4HANA full stack systems but out-of-scope for S/4HANA Foundation systems.

So in that case-

Q1) Does it mean we do not need to do such a check at all for Foundation S4 systems w.r.t note 3049197 ?

Q2) Do we still need to do something "similar" to the readiness check for an Upgrade from a lower ABAP Foundation (say 1709 or 1809) to S/4HANA Foundation 2023 FPS01 version w.r.t note 3049197 ?

Q3) When SUM tool is run then for a S/4HANA Full stack system it will check for SI related items and blocks the Upgrade if those have not  resolved/decided prior to running of SUM, does it means similar is the case for S/4 Foundation systems as well when SUM is run ?

Q4) (Follow-on question to Q3), then instead of readiness check, do  we need still need to run SI checks outside of Readiness check for S4 Foundation upgrade, also do we need to check for  ATC Custom code  checks and are they mandatorily needed for S4 Foundation upgrade ?

 

Note# 3059197 - SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA upgrades

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

15. Can I use SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA upgrades to evaluate an SAP S/4HANA Foundation environment?

SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA upgrades is designed to support full stack SAP S/4HANA systems. The analysis of SAP S/4HANA Foundation systems used for deploying SAP HANA-only Add-Ons is out of scope.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Regards,

Ashish

entry.title: Re: Account determination in A090 for acquisition: down payments and Down-payments clearing account
entry.content: Please note: the Acquisition Down payment GL assigned in AO90 is always an asset reconciliation a/c where as down payment clearing GL is not a reconciliation account but it should be with OIM. A reconciliation GL can't be entered directly. In S/4HANA, we can't post down payment on asset via type 180 in t code FB01/F-02 as it must be posted with a special GL indicator

entry.title: GR and GI Areas for Multiple Plants Linked to a Single Whse: Which storage type to use?
entry.content:
As 10 different plants are connected to one warehouse, each plant having one GR and GI area. Based on plant we need to determine  GR and GI area for inbound and outbound tasks.

Please advise, what type of storage type needs to be created and what configs need to be maintained to achieve the same.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: GR and GI Areas for Multiple Plants Linked to a Singl...
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Please look at the description of each storage type available to create a template to help with this internal decision:

  • Standard storage
    A standard storage type doesn't have any special attributes, as opposed to fixed bin and bulk storage types. The system stores the products in any suitable storage bin. The location of a product in the storage type may change over time as a different product can be stored in a storage bin when it becomes empty. You can't use mixed storage in individual storage bins in the standard storage type.
      You can use handlin
    g units (HUs) in the standard storage type. HUs are optional.
  • Fixed bin storage

In a fixed bin storage type, each product is permanently assigned to one or more storage bins and is always stored there. The assignment must be done using the Assign Fixed Bins app. If a storage bin runs out of stock, the product assignment remains. You mustn't use HUs in this storage type. If you create HUs for a fixed bin storage type, the system deletes them during confirmation of warehouse tasks.

  • Bulk storage
    A bulk storage type is often used for products that are handled in large quantities and therefore take up a large amount of storage space. The advantages of bulk storage include a reduced need for physical storage bins, fast access to the containers, and a clear structuring of the warehouse into blocks and rows. You can't use mixed storage in individual storage bins in the bulk storage type.
    HUs are mandatory in the bulk storage type.
  • General storage
    A general storage type has only one storage bin assigned to it, which allows mixed storage. It's designed for a small number of products.
    Because the general storage type has only one storage bin, it's not intended for a large number of products. The system doesn't know the exact location of the products in the storage bin and can't support you by finding the products in the storage bin.
    You can use HUs in the general storage type. HUs are optional.

Please note:

You can't create or change storage types with the default storage type template. This template is used by SAP Best Practices to create storage types that are needed for certain warehouse processes, such as:

  • Goods receipt
  • Goods issue
  • Clarification
  • Scrapping
  • Production supply

 

To create it on your end, please make sure to perform the configurations for Master Data: Configuration for Master Data.

 

Thank you and best regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: What's New in SAP Operational Workforce Planning
entry.content:
Introduction

Workforce planning is a critical process for any organization that wants to optimize its human capital and align it with its strategic goals. However, workforce planning can be challenging, especially in times of uncertainty and change. That's why we are excited to announce the new release of the Operational Workforce Planning business content package powered by SAP Analytics Cloud, a comprehensive and flexible solution that helps you plan, analyze, and optimize your workforce in an agile and collaborative way.

 

What is SAP Analytics Cloud Business Content?

SAP Analytics Cloud business content is a collection of ready-to-use templates, dashboards, stories, and data models that cover various business scenarios and domains. By using business content, you can jumpstart your analytics journey and leverage best practices and industry standards. You can also customize and extend the business content to suit your specific needs and preferences. SAP Analytics Cloud business content is available for free for all SAP Analytics Cloud customers and partners, and you can browse more information on the SAP Analytics Cloud business content Community Page.

 

What's New in this 2024 Version

The new version of the Operational Workforce Planning business content package offers a number of enhancements and improvements that make it easier, faster, and more intuitive to use. Here are some of the key features and benefits of the new version:

  • Refreshed Look and Feel: The new version follows the SAP Fiori Horizon theme, providing a more seamless user experience when navigating across SAP Success Factors or SAP S/4HANA, and this Workforce Planning business content. The new theme also enhances the readability and aesthetics of the stories and charts.

Refreshed look with more dynamic user interface

 

  • Optimized Stories: The Analytic Apps found in the legacy HXM Operational Workforce Planning have now been updated to Optimized Stories, which provide improved performance and ensure future compatibility with SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • Navigation Menu: A new Navigation menu has been introduced, which improves the user experience when navigating from one story to the next. You can easily access the different planning scenarios, such as top-down aggregated headcount planning or bottom-up detailed headcount planning, as well as the overview, configuration, and reporting  pages.

New menu provides streamlined navigation

 

 

  • Collapsible Side Panels: A new collapsible side panel with multiple drawers has been introduced on the left side of the screen, which maximizes screen real estate to focus on planning activities, while still having convenient access to commonly used elements like filters and help links. You can also access a new comment panel to track threaded discussions during the planning process. On the right side of the screen, you can find another collapsible side panel, which offers the most commonly sought after chart visualizations and corresponding chart filters.

Reusable composite widgets provide dynamic interaction with collapsible side panels

 

  • Redesigned Report Pages: The report pages have been redesigned to align with the requests of several of our premier Workforce Planning implementation partners. You can now view more relevant and actionable information on your workforce planning outcomes and compare various versions against each other.

Redesigned report layouts

 

  • Composites: The new version introduces Composites, which are bespoke reusable components that allow for ease of centralized configurations during implementations. You can use Composites to centrally define and manage common elements used multiple times across stories. This reduces the complexity and maintenance effort of the business content package.

 

Housekeeping Tips

If you have used the HXM Operational Workforce Planning business content package in the past, here are some housekeeping tips to help you transition to the new version:

  • When searching the business content network for this content package, search for “Operational Workforce Planning” or more generically “xP&A” to find this package along with other featured xP&A business content.
  • Our business content folder structure has evolved since HXM Operational Workforce Planning was originally released. If you previously installed the legacy content package, and are now upgrading to this new release, you will want to relocate the Model and Data Actions into the new folders as detailed in the User Help Docs. You can also archive the legacy Analytic Apps, which are now replaced by Optimized Stories.

 

Conclusion

The new release of the Operational Workforce Planning business content package powered by SAP Analytics Cloud is a major milestone in our continuous effort to provide you with the best-in-class solution for workforce planning. With the new features and enhancements, you can plan, analyze, and optimize your workforce with more efficiency, agility, and collaboration. Whether you are a new or existing user of this business content package, we encourage you to explore the new version and discover how it can help you achieve your workforce planning objectives. To learn more about SAP Workforce Planning business content, as well as our other featured Extended Planning and Analysis (xP&A) business content, please visit our xP&A Community Page.

entry.title: Re: We have a requirement to aggregate the data in Techni...
entry.content:
Hi @Venky038, please review Help Potal > SAP6 Sample Planning Area for Demand > Time Profile > WEEKOFQUARTER and MONTHOFQUARTER:

In case the [...] is split across two [...] it needs to be assigned to the [...] to which more days belong to.

Perhaps, this can be inspiration in order for you to define your own custom Time Attribute XYZ, in your Time Profile, you can then maintain the values, by manually (or via an Excel calculation / algorithm) inspecting once in which Technical Week you would like to add the values (you mentioned you are interested in the Technical Week A or B which holds the most days in a ...). After you have determined the appropriate Technical Week via your algorithm, then go ahead and mark/hardcode all the appropriate Technical Weeks for example by maintaining value 1 in your custom Time Attribute XYZ in the Time Profile.

Later, in your Key Figure calculation you can use this Time Attribute in your Key Figure formula. Disaggregation then (or making sure the values are shifted towards the Technical Weeks which have value 1 maintained for your Time Attribute XYZ), you can do, by using a Copy Operator which reads for example on Month or Quarter level, and playing with the Disaggregation settings of the Target Key Figure to only Disaggregate to those values 1 (Note: I always prefer Disaggregation using a Copy Operator, instead of using Disaggregation via Calculated Key Figure split only, since otherwise you end up getting decimals and losing or gaining values, whereas the HANA Disaggregation Engine keeps the totals equal).

Please let me know if it can help, and if not feel free to ask a follow-up question and/or clarify with an exact example and intended outcome.

entry.title: Re: How to use User_Defined Transform in Data Services 4.x using Python Part-1
entry.content:
@kamal264 

Thanks for the detailed blog. There is a tweak to the code which you have used.

 

import re

# Defining local variable
dct = locals()

# Getting salary into local variable
dct[u'SALARY'] = record.GetField(u'SALARY')

# Replace all special characters from Salary to match any character that is not a digit, letter, or underscore.
dct[u'SALARY'] = re.sub(r"[^0-9a-zA-Z_]", "", dct[u'SALARY'])

# Update the output fields Standard Salary
record.SetField(u'STD_SALARY', dct[u'SALARY'])

entry.title: Re: How to show the Planning Periods(Start Date,End Date) those are defined in the Version Dimension
entry.content:
Hi @james_lim 

Unfortunately, this is a not supported & potentially unstable solution because as SAP note 3397906 describes: "Planningmodel getMember API does not support non-generic dimensions (like Version or Organisation). If this API is used mistakenly on a non generic Dimension, it may return incorrect data."

entry.title: spring security intercept pattern override
entry.content:
Hi all!

I'm currently developing an occ extension wich I need to be able to repond in a non secure way (http protocol, port 9001). Now every time I do a call to any of its endpoints, the request is redirected to the ssl port. This happens because a configuration made in security-v2-spring.xml in the "commercewebservices" extension (line 13 down here):

<!-- Swagger resources -->
	<security:http pattern="/v2/swagger-ui*/**" security="none"/>
	<security:http pattern="/v2/api-docs*/**" security="none"/>

	<!-- Commerce Webservices V2-->
	<http pattern="/v2/**" entry-point-ref="oauthAuthenticationEntryPointV2"
	      access-decision-manager-ref="webSecurityAccessDecisionManager"
	      xmlns="http://www.springframework.org/schema/security" create-session="stateless">

		<anonymous username="anonymous" granted-authority="ROLE_ANONYMOUS"/>
		<!--<session-management session-authentication-strategy-ref="fixation"/>-->

		<intercept-url pattern="/**" requires-channel="https"/>

		...
	</http>

My first try was to override the pattern "/v2/**" with this code in <myextension>-web-spring.xml

<security:http pattern="/v2/**" entry-point-ref="oauthAuthenticationEntryPointV2">
        <security:intercept-url pattern="/**" requires-channel="any" />
    </security:http>

 but I got the next error:

GRAVE: Excepción al enviar evento inicializado de contexto a instancia de escuchador de clase [de.hybris.platform.spring.HybrisContextLoaderListener]
org.springframework.beans.factory.BeanCreationException: Error creating bean with name 'defaultCommerceWebServicesFilterChainListV2': Cannot resolve reference to bean 'springSecurityFilterChain' while setting bean property 'sourceList' with key [7]; nested exception is org.springframework.beans.factory.BeanCreationException: Error creating bean with name 'org.springframework.security.filterChainProxy': Invocation of init method failed; nested exception is java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: The FilterChainProxy contains two filter chains using the matcher Ant [pattern='/v2/**']. If you are using multiple <http> namespace elements, you must use a 'pattern' attribute to define the request patterns to which they apply.

The urls of the request is something like http://<host>/occ/v2/<site>/mocks/<anyPath> So I try to make a new pattern:

<security:http pattern="/v2/*/mocks/**" entry-point-ref="oauthAuthenticationEntryPointV2">
    <security:intercept-url pattern="/**" requires-channel="any" />
</security:http>

But the filter spring security creates is inserted at the end of the FilterChainProxy, so OOTB intercept pattern is cheecked first and captured the request.

I think I know what I need, that is to add my pattern before OOTB patterns are added in commercewebservices, but I cannot find a way to do that. Any ideas that could help me with this issue?

 

Thank you in advance.

Ignacio

entry.title: Re: Sessions TechEd Virtual VS Live
entry.content:
My suggestion, prioritize 🙂 It's impossible to follow everything. Many sessions have some overlap which you can detect based on the description. Often sessions which have topics that are close to each other will have overlap and maybe you're not interested in the part that doesn't overlap?!

Priorities for me are:

  1. live is always first priority 🙂
  2. virtual, pick the sessions that are close to your profile to stay up to date
  3. virtual, innovative topics are always good to have in your agenda to learn new things
  4. topics you are interested but not that close or also not that innovative, keep it in your backlog. You can normally watch the virtual once afterwards 😉

Hope this helps a bit!

entry.title: Re: Integrate Document Information Extraction into UI5 application
entry.content:
Hi @prabhatpathania,

Thanks for your comment. I have updated the xs-app.json route as below, and now it's working. In the "target" property, the slash before the "$1" was removed.

        {
            "source": "^/doc-info-extraction/(.*)$",
            "target": "$1",
            "destination": "doc-info-extraction",
            "csrfProtection": false
        },
